parameterdescription.para: The transaction to use for this update

simplesect.para: A Result containing the FastAppend instance or an error

briefdescription.para: Append a data file to this update.

simplesect.para: Reference to this for method chaining

briefdescription.para: Append a manifest file to this update.

detaileddescription.para: The manifest must only contain added files (no existing or deleted files). If the manifest doesn't have a snapshot ID assigned and snapshot ID inheritance is enabled, it will be used directly. Otherwise, it will be copied with the new snapshot ID.

simplesect.para: Reference to this for method chaining

briefdescription.para: A string that describes the action that produced the new snapshot.
Apply the update's changes to the given metadata and snapshot.

parameterdescription.para: The base table metadata to apply changes to
Snapshot to apply the changes to

simplesect.para: A vector of manifest files for the new snapshot

briefdescription.para: Get the summary map for this operation.
Clean up any uncommitted manifests that were created.

detaileddescription.para: Manifests may not be committed if Apply is called multiple times because a commit conflict has occurred. Implementations may keep around manifests because the same changes will be made by both Apply calls. This method instructs the implementation to clean up those manifests and passes the paths of the manifests that were actually committed.

parameterdescription.para: A set of manifest paths that were actually committed

briefdescription.para: Check if cleanup should happen after commit.

simplesect.para: True if cleanup should happen after commit

briefdescription.para: Get the partition spec by spec ID.
Copy a manifest file with a new snapshot ID.
Write new manifests for the accumulated data files.

simplesect.para: A vector of manifest files, or an error

briefdescription.para: Appending new files in a table.

detaileddescription.para: FastAppend is optimized for appending new data files to a table, it creates new manifest files for the added data without compacting or rewriting existing manifests, making it faster for write-heavy workloads.
